contextBlurb: Texas has no enacted AI or data-center law this session, which is notable given how much hyperscale buildout is happening in the state. Pending bills would create sales-tax exemptions for data-center equipment, exempt their energy infrastructure from property tax, and set modest transparency rules for synthetic media.

legislation.title: Relating to the notice requirements of a data broker registration statement and Internet website.
legislation.summary: This bill updates the rules for how data brokers must notify people about their data collection practices, including what information must be posted on their websites. It strengthens transparency requirements for companies that collect and sell personal information.

legislation.title: Relating to the regulation of certain business entities that act as data brokers.
legislation.summary: This bill would create regulations for companies that collect and sell personal data about people, known as data brokers. It sets rules these businesses must follow when handling consumer information.

legislation.title: Relating to an early childhood integrated data system.
legislation.summary: This bill establishes an integrated data system to collect and share information about young children across early childhood programs and services in Texas. It focuses on coordinating child development data but does not impose AI-specific restrictions or incentives.

legislation.title: Relating to the temporary sales and use tax exemptions for certain tangible personal property related to data centers and large data center projects.
legislation.summary: This Texas bill provides temporary sales and use tax exemptions for equipment and materials used in data centers and large data center projects. It is designed to attract data center investment to the state by reducing the tax burden on qualifying purchases.

legislation.title: Relating to the use of automatic license plate reader systems.
legislation.summary: This bill sets rules for how automatic license plate reader systems can be used in Texas, likely covering data collection, storage, and access by law enforcement. It addresses privacy and oversight concerns related to automated vehicle tracking technology.

legislation.title: Relating to the inclusion of provenance data on content shared on social media platforms.
legislation.summary: This bill would require social media platforms to include provenance data — information about the origin and history of content — when material is shared online. The goal is to help users identify the source of digital content, including AI-generated or manipulated media.

legislation.title: Relating to an early childhood integrated data system.
legislation.summary: This bill establishes an integrated data system to collect and share information about young children across early childhood programs in Texas. It focuses on coordinating data to improve early childhood services and outcomes.

legislation.title: Relating to an exemption from ad valorem taxation of the total appraised value of real property used as a data center and the tangible personal property used by the data center to meet the data center's demand for energy.
legislation.summary: This Texas bill would exempt data centers and the equipment they use to meet their energy needs from property taxes. It is designed to attract data center investment to the state by reducing the tax burden on these facilities.
